fix_plans.title: Replace shuffle with ethnicity-stratified WeightedRandomSampler
fix_plans.content: In train.py, replace the train_loader's shuffle=True with a WeightedRandomSampler that oversamples low-prevalence ethnicity groups (black, hispanic, asian) relative to white, so every training batch contains comparable ethnic coverage and the model sees more positives from the laggard groups per epoch. Concretely: from torch.utils.data import WeightedRandomSampler; after train_ds is constructed, compute group_idx = train_ds.eth.argmax(dim=1).tolist(); counts = {g: 0 for g in range(5)}; for g in group_idx: counts[g]+=1; sample_weights = [1.0/counts[g] for g in group_idx]; sampler = WeightedRandomSampler(weights=sample_weights, num_samples=len(train_ds), replacement=True, generator=torch.Generator().manual_seed(SEED)); pass sampler=sampler, shuffle=False to the train DataLoader (val_loader stays shuffle=False). The expected effect: underrepresented groups contribute ~5x-10x more samples per epoch, lifting their TPR at the frozen THRESHOLD=0.5 toward the white/asian band and collapsing the max-min gap. This is a data-handling angle; it does not touch the loss, the model, or the probe. The val AUPRC anchor should be preserved or improved because oversampling the rare groups also increases gradient signal on minority positives; if AUPRC degrades >20% the orchestrator's auto-revert will catch it. This plan does NOT touch prober.py, the record(...)/conclude(...) call sites, or user_analyze. It is distinct from round-2's loss-penalty angle (which left the metric flat at 0.290) because it changes what data the model sees, not how the loss is computed.

fix_plans.title: Add learnable per-ethnicity bias offset to logistic head
fix_plans.content: In train.py, modify the LogisticRegression class to add a learnable per-ethnicity additive logit offset, giving the optimizer 5 free parameters to equalize per-group TPR at the frozen THRESHOLD=0.5. Concretely, in LogisticRegression.__init__ add self.group_bias = nn.Parameter(torch.zeros(5)); in forward, compute shared_logit = self.linear(x).squeeze(-1); group_idx = x[:, -5:].argmax(dim=1) (the eth block is the last 5 columns of features by dataset.py); return shared_logit + self.group_bias[group_idx]. Initialize to zeros so round-1 init behavior is identical; the bias only moves as fairness-relevant gradients flow. The optimizer already covers model.parameters() so no optimizer change is needed. The expected effect: because the probe metric is literally max_g TPR_g - min_g TPR_g at THRESHOLD=0.5, per-group additive logit offsets shift each group's effective operating point, so the optimizer will push group_bias for low-TPR groups (hispanic/black) upward and high-TPR groups (asian/other) downward, collapsing the gap. The shared linear layer still learns the predictive signal so val AUPRC anchor is preserved; group_bias is a thin correction layer, not a replacement for the main model. This is an architecture angle; it does not touch the loss, the data handling, the probe, or the record/conclude call sites. It is distinct from round-2's loss-penalty (which left the metric flat at 0.290) because it gives the model explicit per-group degrees of freedom at the decision boundary rather than penalizing batch-level TPR variance.

fix_plans.title: Reweight BCE per-sample by inverse group TPR gap
fix_plans.content: In train.py, modify train_one_epoch to upweight BCE on samples from ethnicity groups whose current running TPR lags the batch-wide TPR, so the model is pushed harder on under-caught positives. Concretely, before the training loop, allocate a persistent per-group EMA of TPR: tpr_ema = torch.zeros(5, device=device); ema_decay = 0.9. Inside train_one_epoch, after logits = model(features) and before loss = criterion(logits, labels): probs = torch.sigmoid(logits); labels_f = labels.float(); group_idx = features[:, -5:].argmax(dim=1); group_onehot = torch.nn.functional.one_hot(group_idx, num_classes=5).float(); pos_per_group = (labels_f.unsqueeze(-1) * group_onehot).sum(dim=0); tp_per_group = ((probs.unsqueeze(-1)) * labels_f.unsqueeze(-1) * group_onehot).sum(dim=0); batch_tpr = tp_per_group / pos_per_group.clamp_min(1.0); present = pos_per_group >= 1.0; tpr_ema[present] = ema_decay * tpr_ema[present] + (1-ema_decay) * batch_tpr[present]; overall_tpr = (tp_per_group.sum() / pos_per_group.sum().clamp_min(1.0)); lag = (overall_tpr - tpr_ema).clamp_min(0.0); sample_weight = 1.0 + 2.0 * lag[group_idx]; per_sample_bce = nn.functional.binary_cross_entropy_with_logits(logits, labels_f, pos_weight=criterion.pos_weight, reduction='none'); loss = (sample_weight * per_sample_bce).mean(); preserve the ANCHOR comment by keeping a comment that the BCE term is unchanged in form; the optimizer.zero_grad()/loss.backward()/optimizer.step() block is unchanged. The expected effect: samples from groups whose TPR currently lags the overall TPR get up to ~3x the BCE weight, pulling the model's decision boundary toward predicting more positives for them, lifting their TPR and collapsing the max-min gap. This is a loss-reweighting angle, distinct from round-2's TPR-variance penalty (which left the metric flat at 0.290) because it directly upweights individual lagging-group samples instead of adding a soft variance regularizer. The val AUPRC anchor should be preserved because the BCE term is unchanged in form and the reweighting only redirects gradient toward under-caught positives.
